content.description:
Navigation in Hobson determines which item is displayed and how. The system supports both full-page navigation and opening items as siblings within a container.

## URL Structure

The URL reflects the current view state:

```
https://hobson.app/#item-uuid
https://hobson.app/#item-uuid?view=view-uuid
https://hobson.app/?instance=nested#item-uuid
```

| Component | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|
| `#item-uuid` | The root item being displayed |
| `?view=` | Optional view override |
| `?instance=` | Nested instance prefix |

## The Viewport

The [viewport](item://88888888-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) is a special item managing view state:

- **rootId** â€” Currently displayed item
- **rootViewId** â€” Optional view override
- **selection** â€” Currently selected child (runtime only)

Viewport state persists to IndexedDB, so your location survives page refresh.

## Navigation Methods

### Full Navigation: `api.navigate()`

```javascript
// Navigate to an item
api.navigate('item-uuid');

// With view override
api.navigate('item-uuid', { view: 'view-uuid' });

// With scroll target (for code/markdown)
api.navigate('item-uuid', { 
  field: 'code',
  region: 'myFunction',
  line: 42
});
```

This changes the viewport root and updates the URL.

### Sibling Navigation: `api.siblingContainer`

Within a spatial container, items can open as siblings instead of replacing the view:

```javascript
// In a view's render function
if (api.siblingContainer) {
  // Open next to current item
  api.siblingContainer.addSibling('target-uuid');
} else {
  // Not in a container, do full navigation
  api.navigate('target-uuid');
}
```

This is how `item://` links work in markdownâ€”they add siblings when possible.

## Link Syntax

Markdown supports `item://` links:

```markdown
[Display Text](item://item-uuid)
[Code Item](item://item-uuid#code?region=myFunction)
[Line 42](item://item-uuid#code?lines=42)
```

Clicking these links:
1. If inside a container â†’ adds as sibling
2. If at viewport root â†’ full navigation

## Navigation Events

The system emits events on navigation:

- `viewport:root-changed` â€” Root item changed
- `selection:changed` â€” Selected child changed

Subscribe via the [event system](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) to react to navigation.

## Navigation Stack

Browser back/forward buttons work because:
1. Navigation updates `location.hash`
2. `hashchange` event triggers viewport update
3. Previous item renders

## Item Palette (Cmd+K)

The [item search modal](item://f1111111-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) provides quick navigation:

1. Press `Cmd+K` (or `Ctrl+K`)
2. Type to search items
3. Enter to navigate

## See Also

- [kernel:viewport](item://33333333-3333-0000-0000-000000000000) â€” Viewport state management
- [concept:children](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) â€” Parent-child relationships
- [concept:transclusion](item://c0c0c0c0-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) â€” Embedding vs linking
